user.title: Add Bluetooth Device
user.description:
Enter your device's Bluetooth address and choose what type it is.

Factory defaults work for most new devices — expand **Advanced options** only if needed.

data_description.mac_address: The Bluetooth address printed on your device (format: XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX).
data_description.mesh_name: Leave this as the default unless instructed otherwise.
data_description.mesh_password: Leave this as the default unless instructed otherwise.
data_description.vendor_id: Manufacturer identifier in hex. Only change if instructed.

sig_bridge.title: Connect to Device
sig_bridge.description: Enter the address of the device that connects to **{name}**.

data_description.bridge_host: The IP address or hostname of your device (e.g. your Raspberry Pi).
data_description.bridge_port: Port the device is listening on. Default is 8099.

telink_bridge.title: Connect to Device
telink_bridge.description: Enter the address of the device that connects to **{name}**.

data_description.bridge_host: The IP address or hostname of your device (e.g. your Raspberry Pi).
data_description.bridge_port: Port the device is listening on. Default is 8099.

sig_plug.title: Setting Up {name}
sig_plug.description:
We'll automatically set up the secure connection to your device.

Make sure the device is **powered on** and **close to your Bluetooth adapter**, then click **Submit**.

sig_light.title: Import {name}
sig_light.description: Enter the keys and addresses from the existing SIG Mesh network. Keys are stored in Home Assistant's local config-entry storage, which is not encrypted; protect your config directory and backups. Keys are never sent to a cloud service.

data_description.iv_index: The current SIG Mesh IV index.
data_description.adapter: Local BlueZ adapter to own directly, normally hci0.

confirm.title: Add {name}
confirm.description:
**Signal:** {signal_quality}

Select the device type below to finish setup.

reauth_confirm.title: Reconnect Device
reauth_confirm.description: Home Assistant lost connection to your device. Update the settings below to reconnect.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure {name}
reconfigure.description: Update the connection settings for **{name}**. Changes will take effect after the integration restarts.

data_description.mesh_name: The network name your device is paired to.
data_description.mesh_password: The network password your device is paired to.
data_description.bridge_host: The IP address or hostname of your device (e.g. 192.168.1.100).
data_description.bridge_port: Port the device is listening on. Default is 8099.

error.invalid_mac: That doesn't look like a valid Bluetooth address. Expected format: XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX.
error.invalid_key: Invalid key — we need exactly 32 hex characters.
error.invalid_bridge_host: Please enter a valid address, like 192.168.1.100 or raspberrypi.local.
error.invalid_credential_length: Network name and password must each be 16 characters or fewer.
error.invalid_vendor_id: Invalid vendor code — use 1 to 4 hex digits (e.g. 0x1001).
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the device. Check that it's powered on and connected to your network.
error.cannot_connect_ble: Kan inte ansluta. Kontrollera att enheten är i par-mode och nära Bluetooth-adaptern.
error.ble_adapter_busy: Your Bluetooth adapter is busy (status 0x0a). Another integration (e.g., Home Assistant bluetooth) is monopolizing it. To fix this: (1) Add an ESPHome Bluetooth Proxy device, OR (2) Install a second USB Bluetooth adapter. See the integration documentation for details.
error.pairing_failed: Pairing misslyckades. Fabriksåterställ enheten och försök igen.
error.device_type_mismatch: Enhetstypen stämmer inte. Kontrollera att rätt enhetstyp är vald.
error.unknown_device_type: Kunde inte identifiera enhetstyp automatiskt. Välj enhetstyp manuellt.
error.provisioning_failed: Could not connect to the device. Make sure it's powered on and close to your Bluetooth adapter, then try again.
error.timeout: Connection timed out. Make sure the device is powered on and close by, then try again.
error.device_not_found: Device not found. Make sure it's powered on and within Bluetooth range, then try again.
error.invalid_unicast_address: Enter a 4-character hex address between 0001 and 7FFF (e.g. 00B0).
error.provisioning_appkey_failed: Could not bind the application key. Try factory resetting the device, then add it again.
error.provisioning_proxy_failed: Proxy Service step failed. Make sure the device has Proxy Service enabled, then try again.
error.provisioning_pbgatt_failed: PB-GATT connection failed. Move the device closer to your Bluetooth adapter and try again.

abort.already_configured: This device is already set up in Home Assistant.
abort.reauth_successful: Settings updated. Reconnecting your device now.
abort.device_not_available: Device is no longer nearby. Try adding it again when it's in range.
abort.reconfigure_successful: Settings updated. The integration will restart now.
abort.not_in_pairing_mode: Device is not in pairing mode. Reset the device to pairing mode and try again.
abort.invalid_import: The imported SIG Mesh light data is invalid.

init.title: Device Settings
init.description: Update connection settings for this device.

data_description.mesh_name: The network name for your device.
data_description.mesh_password: The network password for your device.
data_description.mesh_address: Internal address of this device. Set to 0 to use the default.
data_description.bridge_host: The IP address or hostname of your device.
data_description.bridge_port: Port the device is listening on. Default is 8099.

connection_failed.message: Could not connect to {device}. Check that it's powered on and within range.

command_timeout.message: No response from {device}. It may be out of range.

provisioning_failed.message: Could not connect to {device}. Try removing and re-adding it.

identify.name: Flash device
identify.description: Flash the device's indicator light to help you identify it.

set_log_level.name: Set log detail
set_log_level.description: Change how much diagnostic detail is logged for this integration. No restart required.

level.name: Level
level.description: Amount of detail: debug (most), info, warning, or error (least).

get_diagnostics.name: Get device info
get_diagnostics.description: Retrieve connection statistics and health information for a device.

provisioning_failed.title: Device setup failed
provisioning_failed.description: Could not set up {device}. Power the device off and on again, then re-add it to Home Assistant.

bridge_unreachable.title: Cannot reach bridge at {host}:{port}
bridge_unreachable.description:
The bridge daemon is not responding.

**Try these steps:**
1. Check that the device device (Raspberry Pi) is **powered on**
2. Verify the device daemon is **running**: `systemctl status tuya-mesh-bridge`
3. Check the **IP address** {host} is correct
4. Test connectivity: `ping {host}`

connection_timeout.title: Cannot connect to {device}
connection_timeout.description:
The device is not responding to Bluetooth connection attempts.

**Try these steps:**
1. **Factory reset** the device (power cycle 3-5 times quickly)
2. **Close** the Tuya Smart / Smart Life app on your phone
3. Make sure the device is **within 5 meters** of your Bluetooth adapter
4. **Restart** Home Assistant

The integration will keep trying to connect automatically.

reconnect_storm.title: Frequent disconnections for {device}
reconnect_storm.description:
The device has reconnected {count} times in the last few minutes. This usually indicates:

- **Weak Bluetooth signal** — move device closer
- **Interference** from other Bluetooth/WiFi devices
- **Power supply issues** — check the device power

The integration will continue reconnecting automatically.

ble_adapter_busy.title: Bluetooth adapter busy — cannot connect to {device}
ble_adapter_busy.description:
Your Bluetooth adapter is busy (error 0x0a). Another integration (likely the built-in Home Assistant Bluetooth integration) is monopolizing the adapter.

**This is a hardware limitation.** To fix it, you have two options:

**Option 1: ESPHome Bluetooth Proxy (Recommended)**
1. Get an ESP32 device (e.g., ESP32-DevKit, M5Atom Lite)
2. Flash it with ESPHome and enable `bluetooth_proxy`
3. Add it to Home Assistant
4. The integration will automatically use it

**Option 2: Second USB Bluetooth Adapter**
1. Buy a USB Bluetooth 4.0+ dongle
2. Plug it into your Home Assistant host
3. Restart Home Assistant
4. Try adding the device again

**Why this happens:** Home Assistant's built-in Bluetooth integration keeps the adapter locked for active scanning. This integration cannot share the same adapter.

See the integration documentation for detailed setup instructions.
